papers.title: Holos: A Web-Scale LLM-Based Multi-Agent System for the Agentic Web
papers.innovation: Proposes Holos, a web-scale LLM-based multi-agent system, marking a pivotal shift towards the 'Agentic Web' where heterogeneous agents autonomously interact and co-evolve. This vision moves LLM agents from isolated task solvers to persistent digital entities, advancing AGI.
papers.benchmarks: Not specified in abstract, focusing on a new system architecture and paradigm.
papers.use_case: Developing foundational infrastructure for the 'Agentic Web', enabling large-scale, co-evolving multi-agent systems and persistent digital entities for various applications.

papers.title: Xpertbench: Expert Level Tasks with Rubrics-Based Evaluation
papers.innovation: Introduces Xpertbench, a novel framework for evaluating LLM proficiency in complex, open-ended tasks that demand expert-level cognition. It addresses the limitations of conventional benchmarks by utilizing a rubrics-based evaluation approach for deeper assessment.
papers.benchmarks: Xpertbench itself is a new evaluation framework/benchmark designed to overcome the plateauing performance assessment of LLMs on conventional benchmarks.
papers.use_case: Rigorously evaluating and comparing LLMs for their ability to perform expert-level tasks, facilitating the development and deployment of more capable and reliable AI systems in specialized domains.

papers.title: AIVV: Neuro-Symbolic LLM Agent-Integrated Verification and Validation for Trustworthy Autonomous Systems
papers.innovation: Proposes an innovative neuro-symbolic LLM agent-integrated framework for Verification and Validation (V&V) of autonomous systems. It addresses the challenges of anomaly classification, scalability, and distinguishing genuine faults from nuisance faults inherent in deep learning models.
papers.benchmarks: Not explicitly mentioned in abstract; focuses on a V&V methodology for trustworthiness rather than specific datasets.
papers.use_case: Ensuring the trustworthiness, reliability, and safety of autonomous systems by providing a robust V&V framework that integrates neuro-symbolic reasoning and LLM agents to accurately classify anomalies and ensure system integrity.
